recentTweets.text:
Congress did something recently that it hasn't done in decades: major housing reform. 

Now, we need acceleration, and not just in housing - cheaper energy, better roads, faster cures, and more. Grateful to talk about it all with @FukuyamaFrancis at the @CNLiberalism Summit! https://t.co/rjmwkcVj4n

Good on North Carolina.

Not every coffee shop, apartment, or small business needs the same amount of parking. Let communities grow based on demand, not outdated formulas that raise costs and waste land.

The U.S. spent $12 billion on transmission congestion costs in 2024 - that's a $33 million tax per day because we've made it too hard to build new transmission.

And it's only going to get worse until we fix our transmission backlog!
https://t.co/t9u3KpPRou

The 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act is finally law! Such a huge step towards tackling our housing shortage & lowering costs. 

But this is also about restoring trust in gov’t and proving that we can solve families’ kitchentable concerns - now we have to keep this momentum going!
Emergencies like this are going to become more and more common as our grid infrastructure ages and new projects stall for years.

Manufactured housing is a critical tool for unlocking affordable options for homebuyers. We just passed reforms to make these homes up to $10k cheaper, alongside so many more pro-growth housing wins. 

Let’s not wait another day for them to become law.
https://t.co/gQPsN1u38o

Indiana is the latest example that when states invest in evidence-based teacher training, students benefit.

Statewide reading scores jumped after Indiana passed science of reading reforms in 2023. More states should be doing this!
https://t.co/duO7FRRHSi

Had a great discussion with @jayparsons on the Rent Roll podcast about critical housing reforms to lower costs and get more homes built faster. Tune in here:
https://t.co/1VTidhmi5u
When we launched last year, we made the case for why getting back to building is so important: https://t.co/bBgrjjBhYH

One year later, it’s tremendous to see the strongest pro-growth, pro-housing package in 50 years finally pass Congress.

I look forward to the President signing this package into law, and to keeping this bipartisan, pro-growth momentum going.

Whether it’s housing costs, or utility bills, or just the potholes in our roads, people are stretched thin in daily life and feeling deep cynicism that things will never change.

This housing package should be just the start of our efforts to restore trust by building.

We need more homes, we need more affordable energy projects, and we need faster fixes for our roads, bridges, and highways.

Unleashing America’s full potential by passing these kinds of pro-growth policies is exactly why the Build America Caucus was founded.

Critically, the final package kept reforms passed by the House in May to fix build-to-rent provisions that would cut 72k new units every year.

This was the biggest sticking point of this saga & the Build America Caucus worked around the clock to fix it.
https://t.co/7q9da8fNXY

But this package is more than just a massive affordability win after decades of housing inaction by Congress.

Passing these kinds of historic, pro-growth policies is proof that government can still make progress on addressing the kitchen table problems families feel every day.

The 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act will lower costs, jumpstart new construction, cut red tape, and speed up some of the slowest parts of approving new housing.

It includes a $200 million Innovation Fund, manufactured housing reforms, and policies to help cities grow faster.

It also brings back reforms that were left out during months of negotiations.

The Build Now Act is back, which rewards communities that improve their homebuilding rate.

The final package also includes funding for maintaining and improving manufactured housing communities.

That’s why passing a historic housing package to lower costs this Congress is so important. 

For months, it felt like we were on the verge of fumbling at the five-yard-line.

But with today’s passage (358-32!), we finally scored a game-winning touchdown.

Housing has been unaffordable for a long time. Home prices have nearly quadrupled since the 1940s when adjusted for inflation.

Congress passed Section 8 housing in the 70s to give low-income families some relief, but Washington has been hitting the snooze button ever since.

The modern-day impacts of that inaction have been severe:

Nearly one-in-three young adults now live with their parents.
The average homebuyer has to wait until their 40s to buy their first home.
Almost half of all renters spend 30% of their paychecks on rent.

Congress has tried passing a housing affordability package half a dozen times over the last eight months. 

With the Build America Caucus’ help, today is the first time one is heading to the President’s desk. Here’s how we got to this massive win.🧵
One step closer to finally becoming law!

Paying companies to kill new energy projects is not going to solve our energy crisis.
https://t.co/eUCQbNuXgh
We have to make it easier to build the things we need. https://t.co/S5kAsMlpAt

RT @ThomasHochman: The FREEDOM Act is out in the Senate today with bipartisan cosponsors @SenTomCotton & @SenCortezMasto.

This bill would…

In February, I announced the FREEDOM Act in the House with the Build America Caucus. I'm super excited to see it being introduced in the Senate — looking forward to getting politics out of permitting and lowering costs for our families!

If you’re happy with energy prices right now, then we don’t need permit reform.

But if you feel like the status quo isn’t working, then we need to cut the costly red tape holding down new projects and holding up affordability for families. https://t.co/u2MYIdbRbW

We can’t let politics get in the way of building more energy supply. 
 
Glad to sit down with @ThomasHochman to discuss the FREEDOM Act and unlocking America’s full energy potential. https://t.co/FGCJZD0I69

The literacy crisis is real, and we have to act before it’s too late. That’s why I’m pushing to restore the National Reading Panel to jumpstart evidence-based solutions for the next generation of students. https://t.co/U39Xjww0vP

Over/under — how long is this going to take to get approved? 

Without serious reforms that make it easier to build, see you at the groundbreaking in 2060!

A country that can't build enough homes, enough power, enough infrastructure, enough innovation, and enough opportunity is going to struggle to maintain public trust. That’s why I created the Build America Caucus, so we can get back to building again. https://t.co/2Fz7t3bIdA

AI is an exciting tool for accelerating innovation, but it’s also exposing risks in our biotech industry.

Requiring supply chain safeguards to stop bioweapons from ending up in the hands of bad actors should be a no-brainer.
Making it easier to get more geothermal built faster is a huge win. More good-paying jobs, lower energy costs, and greater energy security - that’s what we’re unlocking with today’s reforms.

Doubling survival times for one of the deadliest kinds of cancer is a massive achievement. 

So excited for more breakthrough science like this in the future!
We’re seeing pro-growth policies gain traction with liberals and conservatives alike because scarcity is rampant in every community in America. So cool to see New York making building a priority!

Just about the only thing Americans agree on these days is that our country is broken - fixing that means getting back to building. 

Had a blast talking to @makosloff on the @RealignmentPod about the Build America Caucus’ work to restore trust in gov’t.
https://t.co/oZ9yLCbTqh
Passing the largest housing legislation in 50 years is just the start. We have to continue cutting costly red tape and get more homes on the market ASAP! https://t.co/QowpVsRBx8
More states should be doing this!

After decades of inaction, yesterday’s housing package would be a huge step towards fixing our housing crisis.

Loved sitting down with @the535news to talk about the Build America Caucus’ work to get more housing built! https://t.co/NGpbWWF4Qc
Today is International Clinical Trials Day. We have to speed up the trial process and get life saving treatments onto the market.

Our families have waited long enough. Washington has ignored our housing crisis for years.
 
The best time to address this problem was decades ago. The next best time is right now.
 
Let’s get this done.

That all means this package, which is the strongest federal housing package in half a century, is now the most durable path for historic housing reforms becoming law. 
 
Now the Senate needs to make passing this their top priority.

I just voted to pass the strongest housing bill in 50 years. It passed the House with overwhelming support, as well as UNANIMOUS support from the Build America Caucus! 
 
Now I’m calling on the Senate to fast-track this package to the President’s desk.🧵 https://t.co/LDd59OXfkM

NEWS: I’m excited to officially endorse the amended housing package with Build America Caucus Co-Chair @RepMaloyUtah!

This is the strongest pro-housing package in 50 years, and I’m looking forward to voting to pass these historic reforms this week.
Huge news! We should be doing this at the National Institutes of Health too!

Fixing provisions that threatened our housing goals is exactly what we pushed for with 75 bipartisan Members just a few weeks ago - so excited it worked!

Let’s get this across the finish line!

Incredibly exciting news, especially at a time when people are more pessimistic than ever that life will ever be affordable again. 

The key to restoring hope lies in building more!

Everyone agrees we need housing reform. Let’s find a compromise on a package that unquestionably increases our housing supply. 

The Build America Caucus will continue to work to pass a deal that delivers on our nationwide housing needs.
The best time to streamline nuclear energy was 70 years ago - the next best time is right now!
You don’t say!
See my bill to suspend the gas tax thru the end of the year! https://t.co/S0AVcu6hkk https://t.co/6Dekac1YOt
Always looking to cut the red tape either in the government or cake. https://t.co/i0q7qaHw1D
We’ve also paved the way for energy permitting certainty which would build out the American energy grid so America can finally be an energy leader.

One year in, the mission is clear:
 
We’re here to make sure we build America.

One year ago, we launched the Build America Caucus with a simple idea:
 
America should build things again. 🧵
Happy one year anniversary to the Build America Caucus. We have accomplished so much in just one year thanks to our amazing members! https://t.co/N6XPngo01g
RT @hapgoodreports: Sat down with @RepJoshHarder to talk about how the new caucus he co-leads has pushed bipartisan housing priorities as C…
Section 901 is the wrong answer to the right question. Here’s the right answer: https://t.co/Mf5X1LLQ7e
Exciting movement here. The two worst outcomes in the housing fight are passing a bill that effectively bans build-to-rent housing, or passing nothing at all. Let’s find a better option!

If the Millennium Falcon had to go through California permitting, Han would STILL be waiting on his light-speed-impact study. 

Happy May the 4th!

kurdistanMentions.text:
Congress did something recently that it hasn't done in decades: major housing reform. 

Now, we need acceleration, and not just in housing - cheaper energy, better roads, faster cures, and more. Grateful to talk about it all with @FukuyamaFrancis at the @CNLiberalism Summit! https://t.co/rjmwkcVj4n

We need more homes, we need more affordable energy projects, and we need faster fixes for our roads, bridges, and highways.

Unleashing America’s full potential by passing these kinds of pro-growth policies is exactly why the Build America Caucus was founded.

Paying companies to kill new energy projects is not going to solve our energy crisis.
https://t.co/eUCQbNuXgh

If you’re happy with energy prices right now, then we don’t need permit reform.

But if you feel like the status quo isn’t working, then we need to cut the costly red tape holding down new projects and holding up affordability for families. https://t.co/u2MYIdbRbW

We can’t let politics get in the way of building more energy supply. 
 
Glad to sit down with @ThomasHochman to discuss the FREEDOM Act and unlocking America’s full energy potential. https://t.co/FGCJZD0I69
Making it easier to get more geothermal built faster is a huge win. More good-paying jobs, lower energy costs, and greater energy security - that’s what we’re unlocking with today’s reforms.
The best time to streamline nuclear energy was 70 years ago - the next best time is right now!
We’ve also paved the way for energy permitting certainty which would build out the American energy grid so America can finally be an energy leader.

The age of electricity shouldn't be defined by utility bills higher than your monthly rent.

As AI demand increases, it's more important than ever we reduce the yearslong grid connectivity backlog and make it easier to build new affordable energy projects.

These shocking stories do more than illustrate how red tape drives rising prices. People lose trust in gov’t when unfindable salamanders are killing energy projects.

Grateful for @JoinFAI & others work to call out this insanity - now we need to meet this moment with real change.

Here’s something you won’t hear a lot of Democrats say: nuclear energy is something that the Trump Admin is getting right and California is getting wrong. 

We need more affordable energy, not less, and nuclear should be part of our future energy toolbelt.

assessment.summary: The captured X archive has 11 Kurdistan Lens tweet hits for Josh Harder. The social-media posture reads as constructive or diplomatic signal because the strongest matched frame is support, partnership, or official-contact language. Top matched terms: Oil / Energy.
assessment.whatItWasLike: When this account mentioned the Kurdish file, the language most often clustered around support, partnership, or official-contact language. I count 0 direct Kurdistan/Kurdish-region term hits, 1 security-frame hit, 4 supportive or diplomatic hits, 0 humanitarian/minority hits, and 11 energy/economic hits in the exported profile window. The latest matched tweet is dated 2026-07-16T17:41:07Z and should be opened before treating this as a final stance.

One word: TheyIncreasedTheHousingSupply

The age of electricity shouldn't be defined by utility bills higher than your monthly rent.

As AI demand increases, it's more important than ever we reduce the yearslong grid connectivity backlog and make it easier to build new affordable energy projects.
RT @TahraHoops: NEW: Excited to have @RepJoshHarder back on the Rebuild with @GaryWinslett to discuss the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act…

Halting build-to-rent housing during a housing crisis is like trying to solve a food shortage by banning supermarkets.

Talked with @TahraHoops and @GaryWinslett on The Rebuild on why build-to-rent is so important.

Check out the full episode here: https://t.co/uyUCIiALNM https://t.co/GKRMFGhCmQ
We can’t let one section tank the odds of the best housing legislation in a generation. https://t.co/PJuaGCpmeU
RT @TahraHoops: Section 901 of the ROAD to Housing Act would block ~72,000 new rental units every year by effectively halting build-to-rent…

Increased supply by tying funding to production, faster reviews and less paperwork, modern manufactured housing rules. An Innovation Fund that incentivizes local communities that take new approaches to housing.
 
This package does so much to modernize housing in America.

But the two worst outcomes possible in this fight are passing this bill with Section 901 included, or not passing anything at all.
 
Let’s go with door number 3 - reworking Section 901 & fast-tracking a deal that will unlock affordable housing nationwide.

We joined forces with the bipartisan, congressional Real Estate Caucus to highlight the damage to renters, builders, and communities, as well as the viability of historic housing reforms, if Section 901 remains included.
 
The bottom line is 99% of this package is great.

🚨We’ve never been closer to stripping the most problematic provision out of the Senate housing package.
 
Here’s what you need to know:🧵 https://t.co/xBU64wq8ns

Secretaries putting their thumb on the scale for their favorite projects (and actively blocking others) is not sustainable policy. We need to level the permitting playing field. https://t.co/u1w0STu8jN

RFK talks a big game on stopping pollution, but he just admitted he has zero backbone as Trump doubles air and water pollution.

Grow a spine and stand up for Americans’ health.
RT @TheCalGuard: Thank you to Congressman Josh Harder for visiting our cadets yesterday — and for his steadfast commitment to ensuring Disc…

Section 901 is a major legislative flaw nobody asked for. The rest is the strongest pro-housing package in a generation — funding tied to production, manufactured housing, opportunity zones. We have to get this right for the future of American housing.

30% increase in housing stock, followed by a median $250 drop in rent.

This is what’s possible when we build, and why it’s so important that we make it easier to build now so we’re not left behind in the future.

I want to use AI to innovate: cure cancer, speed up science, & build more. But as AI makes cyberattacks easier, we need defensive innovation to protect our grid & critical infrastructure. America’s lead in AI capabilities should be used to cement our security, not undermine it.

These shocking stories do more than illustrate how red tape drives rising prices. People lose trust in gov’t when unfindable salamanders are killing energy projects.

Grateful for @JoinFAI & others work to call out this insanity - now we need to meet this moment with real change.

We just sent four people further into space than we ever had before. That project started in 2017. 

The California High Speed Rail project started in 2015 and we only have a massive construction mess to show for it. Houston, we have a red tape problem.

You can't make this stuff up.

The environmental review process was supposed to protect nature. Instead, it flooded communities with sewage. 

Talk about a bureaucratic permitting failure.

Very exciting new tool that underscores how expensive utilities have gotten. 

If Congress does its job, we should be able to look at this map a year from now and see a lot more green!
BREAKING: The Build America Caucus has received all necessary permits to tweet this message. Review took 14 months. Happy #AprilFools 🏗️ (This is why we need permitting reform.)

Here’s something you won’t hear a lot of Democrats say: nuclear energy is something that the Trump Admin is getting right and California is getting wrong. 

We need more affordable energy, not less, and nuclear should be part of our future energy toolbelt.
